title: Trentalle Sancti Gregorii
summary: The checked-in CME00102 XML is a repository copy of a University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English encoding derived from Karl Daniel Bülbring's 1891 Anglia article on the anonymous B-version of the Trentalle Sancti Gregorii in National Library of Scotland Adv.MS.19.3.1, ff. 213r-216r. Bülbring directly inspected and transcribed the manuscript, corrected William Turnbull's 1843 printing, and consulted Albert Kaufmann's 1889 version study. The XML starts at Bülbring's printed p. 303, omitting the article's pp. 301-302 introduction and Turnbull correction list, and retains the 190-position poem, twenty footnotes, a separately indexed two-line post-explicit scribal saying, and German commentary through p. 308. A complete 482-canvas NLS IIIF bundle maps the target to canvases 441-447; canvas 448 is adjacent f. 216v music and is outside the work. DIMEV v1.0 distinguishes the four IMEV 3184 B-version witnesses and the two-line saying, while Kaufmann and current scholarship distinguish other Trental versions. Complete public source objects are cached where obtainable, including the NLS IIIF object, Anglia volume, DIMEV release, BSB Kaufmann dissertation object, and Gordon's 2026 open-access article; the cached Internet Archive Kaufmann book scan is explicitly partial because printed pp. 18-25 are absent.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00102
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00102
entities.description: Electronic encoding of Bülbring's printed pp. 303-308. It preserves the article title, 190-position poem, italic typography, twenty footnotes, page and manuscript-folio milestones, explicit, separate post-explicit saying, and eleven German commentary paragraphs, but excludes Bülbring's introduction and Turnbull correction list on pp. 301-302.

entities.label: Karl Daniel Bülbring, Das Trentalle Sancti Gregorii in der Edinburgher Handschrift (1891)
entities.description: Bülbring's eight-page Anglia article: pp. 301-302 introduce his 1890 direct inspection of the Edinburgh codex and enumerate Turnbull's errors; pp. 303-308 print the complete Adv.MS.19.3.1 poem and its post-explicit saying with footnotes and German Anmerkungen. Bülbring also compares Kaufmann, a Cambridge witness he calls C, Lambeth, and Vernon readings.

bibliographic.citation: Karl Daniel Bülbring, 'Das Trentalle Sancti Gregorii in der Edinburgher Handschrift,' Anglia: Zeitschrift für englische Philologie 13, Neue Folge 1 (1891): 301-308.
bibliographic.title: Das Trentalle Sancti Gregorii in der Edinburgher Handschrift

entities.label: National Library of Scotland Adv.MS.19.3.1
entities.description: A c. 1480 paper codex of nine originally independent fascicles, principally copied by Richard Heeg, with the Trental on ff. 213r-216r among approximately contemporary additions by another, unidentified hand. The poem ends with a Latin explicit and a two-line fidelity saying; the adjacent f. 216v contains the musical hymn Deus creator omnium.

date_statements.label: Target addition
date_statements.value: approximately contemporary with the main codex

holding.notes: Former Advocates Library manuscript, presented to the nation on the National Library of Scotland's foundation in 1925.

notes: The target is an addition by a hand other than principal scribe Richard Heeg.
Provider notes say numerous modern blank flyleaves are not all represented in the digital sequence.

entities.label: NLS digital facsimile of Adv.MS.19.3.1
entities.description: The complete provider-published IIIF Presentation 2 object with 482 numbered canvases. The public object omits some modern blank flyleaves by design; the Trental occupies canvases 441-447 and adjacent canvas 448 is f. 216v music outside the work.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and color page images

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Anglia volume 13 (1891)
entities.description: Complete 558-page PDF and complete-volume OCR of the 1891 Anglia volume digitized from the University of Toronto Robarts Library copy; Bülbring's article occupies PDF pp. 315-322, and the CME-encoded portion begins at PDF p. 317.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete-volume PDF, OCR text, and item metadata

entities.label: William Barclay D. D. Turnbull, The Visions of Tundale (1843)
entities.description: Turnbull's scarce Edinburgh volume prints the Adv.MS.19.3.1 Trental on pp. 77-83. Bülbring states that it was limited to 105 copies and lists numerous misreadings, unnecessary changes, and corrections before supplying his new direct transcription.

bibliographic.citation: William Barclay David Donald Turnbull, ed., The Visions of Tundale (Edinburgh: T. G. Stevenson, 1843), 77-83.
bibliographic.title: The Visions of Tundale

entities.label: Albert Kaufmann, Trentalle Sancti Gregorii (1889 expanded publication)
entities.description: Kaufmann's 57-page Erlanger Beiträge publication distinguishes A and B versions. For B, he describes Edinburgh E as a 190-line copy with wholly missing lines 100 and 150 and Cambridge C as a 202-line copy, hypothesizes independent descent from a lost antecedent, and prints C because his E evidence came through Turnbull and J. Clark's collation rather than a reliable direct transcription.

bibliographic.citation: Albert Kaufmann, Trentalle Sancti Gregorii: Eine mittelenglische Legende in zwei Texten, Erlanger Beiträge 3 (Erlangen and Leipzig: A. Deichert'sche Verlagsbuchhandlung Nachf. [Georg Böhme], 1889).
bibliographic.title: Trentalle Sancti Gregorii: Eine mittelenglische Legende in zwei Texten

entities.label: Internet Archive / Google scan of Kaufmann's 1889 expanded publication
entities.description: A 70-page provider PDF that is not physically complete: the scan jumps from printed p. 17 to p. 26. It does preserve the title matter, pp. 1-17, and pp. 26-57, including the printed B-version Cambridge text and notes, and is therefore cached with partial coverage.

entities.label: Albert Kaufmann, Trentalle Sancti Gregorii (1889 dissertation issue)
entities.description: The 49-page dissertation issue represented by BSB bsb11663735. It contains the B-version discussion on printed pp. 19-24 and Cambridge text on pp. 44-49 but not the expanded publication's pp. 50-57 notes; it is modeled separately rather than treated as an identical physical copy of the 57-page Erlanger Beiträge issue.

bibliographic.citation: Albert Kaufmann, Trentalle Sancti Gregorii: Eine mittelenglische Legende in zwei Texten (doctoral dissertation, Friedrich-Alexander-Universität Erlangen, 1889).
bibliographic.title: Trentalle Sancti Gregorii: Eine mittelenglische Legende in zwei Texten

entities.label: BSB digital facsimile of Kaufmann's 1889 dissertation issue
entities.description: Complete 68-canvas IIIF object for BSB shelfmark P.o.angl.34 t-3/5, including covers and the entire 49-page dissertation. Printed pp. 19-24 map to canvases 27-32 and pp. 44-49 to canvases 52-57.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and page images

entities.label: DIMEV v1.0 records 4979 and 762
entities.description: Versioned scholarly descriptions of IMEV/NIMEV 3184 and the separately indexed post-explicit saying IMEV 467.5. Record 4979 lists four B-version witnesses, title, incipits and explicits, 101-couplet tradition-level extent, aa form, and editions; record 762 distinguishes the two-line saying and its three Adv.MS.19.3.1 occurrences.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Kk.1.6
entities.description: B-version witness at ff. 242v-245v, ending with the scribal explicit 'Explicit quod Rycardus ffox.' DIMEV records a 202-line form; Kaufmann calls this C and prints it from a transcript supplied by Rogers rather than from the Edinburgh witness.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 3810, Part I
entities.description: B-version witness on ff. 76v-84v, separately described by DIMEV and the current British Library catalog. It is collateral to, not the copy text of, Bülbring's Edinburgh edition.

entities.label: National Library of Wales Brogyntyn MS II.6
entities.description: A first-half-fifteenth-century parchment codex containing the Pricke of Conscience, this B-version Trental at ff. 94v-96, and seven penitential psalms, written in anglicana formata by one scribe. The catalog's dialect localization concerns the Pricke text and is not automatically transferred to the Trental.

date_statements.label: Manuscript production
date_statements.value: first half of the fifteenth century

holding.notes: Original access restricted; readers are directed to surrogates.

notes: Damp, mould, and mouse damage with only minor loss at the top lines.

entities.label: Phillipa Hardman, The Heege Manuscript (2000)
entities.description: Published paper facsimile of NLS Adv.MS.19.3.1, documented by the NLS catalog and DIMEV. No current open online copy or direct purchase route was verified during this review.

bibliographic.citation: Phillipa Hardman, intro., The Heege Manuscript: A Facsimile of National Library of Scotland MS Advocates 19.3.1, Leeds Texts and Monographs, n.s. 16 (Leeds: School of English, 2000).
bibliographic.title: The Heege Manuscript: A Facsimile of National Library of Scotland MS Advocates 19.3.1
bibliographic.series: Leeds Texts and Monographs, new series 16

entities.label: NLS microfilm Mf.Sec.MSS.173
entities.description: Microfilm reproduction listed for Adv.MS.19.3.1; the public catalog does not state its exact surface coverage.

entities.label: NLS microfilm Mf.Sec.MSS.549
entities.description: Microfilm reproduction listed for Adv.MS.19.3.1; the public catalog does not state its exact surface coverage.

entities.label: NLS microfilm Mf.Sec.MSS.699
entities.description: Microfilm reproduction listed for Adv.MS.19.3.1; the NLS catalog explicitly warns that this film may not be complete.

entities.label: Stephen Gordon, Conjuring the Dead in St Erkenwald (2026)
entities.description: Current open-access scholarly article used for its work-specific distinction between the A-version, B-versions such as Brogyntyn II.6, and the later C-version in print, and for its treatment of the Trental's purgatorial ghost and suffrage motifs.

bibliographic.citation: Stephen Gordon, 'Conjuring the Dead in St Erkenwald,' Review of English Studies 77, no. 330 (June 2026): 286-299.
bibliographic.title: Conjuring the Dead in St Erkenwald

rights.component: NLS digital page images and item-level facsimile

The article was published in 1891, and the Internet Archive item metadata reports NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT in the United States; current U.S. duration guidance places such publication outside copyright.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan derivatives and metadata
rights.basis: Exact item metadata reports possible copyright NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT and no visible notice for the 1891 volume; this does not itself establish worldwide status or an open license for every derivative.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript abbreviations, ambiguous letters, corrections, and supplied readings
editorial_practices.representation: Italic letters and spans, brackets, sic notes, and footnotes
editorial_practices.description: Bülbring's typography records editorial expansions and supplies but is not a diplomatic image of the manuscript signs; the XML preserves 347 printed italic HI elements without reconstructing the original marks.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Adv.MS.19.3.1 direct copy, Turnbull's prior printing, Kaufmann, Cambridge C, Lambeth, and Vernon readings
editorial_practices.representation: Edinburgh witness text with copy-specific corrections and comparative readings in German footnotes and Anmerkungen
editorial_practices.description: The main text remains a direct Edinburgh transcription; collateral readings are kept in apparatus rather than silently producing a composite critical text.
editorial_practices.scope: Whole article, especially pp. 301-302 and p. 308

editorial_practices.source_feature: Eight-page article with a two-page introduction and six pages of encoded text and commentary
editorial_practices.representation: Encoding starts at printed p. 303 and ends at p. 308
editorial_practices.description: The XML omits Bülbring's pp. 301-302 manuscript introduction and detailed Turnbull correction list while retaining the complete edited poem, post-explicit saying, footnotes, and Anmerkungen.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A 190-position Trental followed by a two-line fidelity saying and German commentary
editorial_practices.representation: The saying is encoded as EPIGRAPH/Q and the commentary as a second DIV1
editorial_practices.description: Structural inclusion does not merge the separately indexed saying or modern commentary into the anonymous medieval Trental. The XML count of 192 L therefore must be decomposed into 190 Trental positions plus two saying lines.

evidence.locator: TEMPHEAD, IDG, TEXT, and complete BODY
evidence.summary: Direct local evidence for identifiers, repository artifact, Bülbring-derived content, 190-position poem, post-explicit saying, notes, commentary, pagination, foliation milestones, and typography.

evidence.citation: Reproducible XML element, attribute, and source-boundary audit of CME00102.xml.
evidence.locator: ETS/EEBO/TEXT direct counts and comparison with Anglia pages
evidence.summary: Confirms 22,434 bytes; 2 DIV1; 2 HEAD; 1 LG; 192 L; 11 P; 20 NOTE; 6 PB; 55 MILESTONE; 2 CLOSER; 1 TRAILER; 1 EPIGRAPH; 1 Q; 347 HI; and encoded printed pp. 303-308. The first 190 L form the Trental lineation and the final two the separate saying.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English item CME00102.
evidence.summary: Stable official item identity. Current automated access encountered a Cloudflare challenge, so structural and content claims are independently grounded in the checked-in XML and source edition.

evidence.citation: Karl Daniel Bülbring, 'Das Trentalle Sancti Gregorii in der Edinburgher Handschrift,' Anglia 13 (1891): 301-308.

The manifest says some modern blank flyleaves were not captured by the provider.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland, Copyright guidance.

This general page does not replace the exact item's Public Domain Mark statement and does not turn unrelated NLS web content into PDM material.

evidence.summary: Defines the mark as identifying material free of known copyright restrictions while preserving jurisdictional and other-rights caveats; it is not a license.

evidence.citation: Albert Kaufmann, Trentalle Sancti Gregorii: Eine mittelenglische Legende in zwei Texten (1889).
evidence.locator: version B discussion pp. 19-24 in BSB object; Cambridge B-version text pp. 44-49; notes pp. 50-57 in partial IA PDF

Clark, and his refusal to date version B more closely than its fifteenth-century witnesses. The cited IA cache is partial and paired with the complete BSB dissertation cache.

evidence.locator: 68 canvases; printed pp. 19-24 = canvases 27-32; printed pp. 44-49 = canvases 52-57

evidence.citation: National Library of Wales, Brogyntyn MS II.6, 'The Pricke of Conscience, &c.'

The Pricke dialect statement is not transferred to the Trental.

evidence.citation: Delivery audit of the NLW catalog-listed Brogyntyn MS II.6 digital handle.
evidence.summary: On 2026-07-13 the exact catalog-listed handle returned HTTP 500 through Cloudflare in both browser extraction and command-line header checks. The dated failure blocks a complete cache now but does not establish that the facsimile has been withdrawn permanently.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 3810, record 040-002049643.
evidence.summary: Official current catalog evidence for the collateral Harley B-version witness and its ff. 76v-84v locus.

evidence.citation: Stephen Gordon, 'Conjuring the Dead in St Erkenwald,' Review of English Studies 77.330 (2026): 286-299.

evidence.summary: Current evidence explicitly distinguishing the A-version, B-versions such as Brogyntyn II.6, and later C-version in print; contextualizes purgatorial ghosts, confession, and suffrage; and supplies exact CC BY 4.0 terms for the complete article.

evidence.citation: Creative Commons, Attribution 4.0 International license deed and legal code.
evidence.summary: Defines attribution requirements and reuse permissions for DIMEV v1.0 and Gordon's article where each provider applies the license.

evidence.citation: United States Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.

open_questions.question: When and where was the B-version composed, what source or sources underlie it, and what exact stemma connects its four surviving witnesses?
next_steps: Consult Martin Connolly's 2011 Brogyntyn edition and subsequent Trental scholarship.
Obtain and collate complete facsimiles of CUL Kk.1.6, BL Harley 3810 Part I, NLW Brogyntyn II.6, and NLS Adv.MS.19.3.1.
Treat Kaufmann's lost B antecedent and possible oral transmission as hypotheses until tested against all witnesses.

open_questions.question: Was the f. 216r two-line fidelity saying written by the Trental scribe or by a different hand?
next_steps: Inspect the physical manuscript or preservation masters at higher resolution.
Compare the letterforms and ink with the parallel sayings on ff. 7 and 157v.
Retain Bülbring's same-hand and DIMEV's different-hand statements as attributed conflicting claims until resolved.

open_questions.question: Can complete, reusable color facsimiles of the three collateral B-version manuscripts be obtained and cached with exact work mappings?
next_steps: Recheck or report the NLW handle's HTTP 500 and cache the complete Brogyntyn codex if service returns.
Search current Cambridge and British Library delivery systems before ordering new whole-object photography.
Request explicit repository, publication, and print-on-demand reuse terms for each supplied image set.

open_questions.question: Do NLS microfilms 173 and 549 cover the complete codex, exactly what is missing from microfilm 699, and can preservation masters include every modern blank flyleaf omitted from public IIIF?
next_steps: Ask NLS for reel-level coverage statements and whether duplication is available.
Specify covers, spine, pastedowns, all flyleaves and blanks, every leaf, edges if offered, and separate target mapping in any whole-object request.
Keep the complete provider-published IIIF bundle distinct from a complete physical-surface reproduction.

open_questions.question: What current item-level access and reuse statement applies to the U-M CME00102 encoding?
next_steps: Retry the stable U-M URL in an ordinary browser after the Cloudflare challenge clears.
Capture the exact item or collection rights statement without applying it backward to the NLS images or Bülbring scan.
Continue grounding content claims in the checked-in XML and source edition.

review_notes: The primary path is repository XML to U-M encoding to Bülbring's 1891 direct transcription to NLS Adv.MS.19.3.1, ff. 213r-216r.
Bülbring used the physical manuscript directly; Turnbull is a corrected prior edition and Kaufmann is a mediated comparison source, not a replacement source witness.
The CUL, BL, NLW, and NLS copies are four collateral witnesses of DIMEV record 4979 / IMEV 3184. Their distinct extents and readings are not collapsed into the 190-position Edinburgh version.

DIMEV's 101-couplet record description belongs to the broader witness family. The XML's 192 L elements decompose into 190 Trental positions, including two missing-line rows, plus a separately indexed two-line saying.
Canvas mapping was visually corrected: canvases 441-447 are ff. 213r-216r and contain the work; canvas 448 is f. 216v music outside the work.
Richard Heeg is the codex's principal scribe but not the target Trental scribe according to the current NLS catalog.
NLS item images carry an exact Public Domain Mark 1.0 statement; BSB Kaufmann images carry NoC-NC 1.0; DIMEV and Gordon are CC BY 4.0. These statements remain attached to their exact access layers.

All publicly obtainable supporting files used intensively were cached whole where possible and hard-linked into the main checkout's ignored source cache. The IA Kaufmann PDF is correctly recorded as partial despite its pre-existing filename, and the NLW complete digital handle could not be cached because it returned HTTP 500.
